vllm.title: [Bugfix][CI][CPU] add missing datasets package to requirements-cpu.txt
vllm.author: bigPYJ1151

vllm.title: [Frontend] Separate pooling APIs in offline inference
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR builds on #10820, splitting up the LLM.encode() API according to the model's task. This avoids confusion about the data format when accessing the pooler output. The pooling-related APIs are:

[UPDATED] LLM.encode(): Returns the raw tensor, instead of a nested list of floats
[NEW] LLM.embed()

vllm.title: [V1][VLM] Fix edge case bug for InternVL2
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
When there's only one image in the batch, the image_embeds needs to be reshaped to [feature_size, hidden_size] from [num_patches, feature_size_per_patch, hidden_size] before getting unsqueezed.
This was previously not observed during offline mode because the default max_num_batched_tokens=8196 is al

vllm.title: [Refactor]A simple device-related refactor
vllm.author: noemotiovon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: A simple device-related refactor: abstract is_pin_memory_available into individual Platform classes, reducing the use of if-else statements and improving maintainability.

vllm.title: [Core] support LoRA and prompt adapter in content-based hashing for Block Manager v2 prefix caching
vllm.author: llsj14
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Block Manager v2, unlike v1, did not support LoRA and prompt adapter for the block hash in prefix caching mode.
I added logic to inject the LoRA ID and prompt adapter ID into the block hash function to support LoRA and prompt adapter while using prefix caching mode with block manager v2.
Det

vllm.title: [Bugfix] using len(tokenizer) instead of tokenizer.vocab_size in AllowedTokenIdsLogitsProcessor
vllm.author: zhangjf-nlp
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
As defined in tokenization_utils_fast.py, len(tokenizer) includes the added tokens but tokenizer.vocab_size does not.
    @property
    def __len__(self) -> int:
        """
        Size of the full vocabulary with the added tokens.
        """
        return self._tokenizer.get_vocab_size(with_adde

vllm.title: [Misc] Add tokenizer_mode param to benchmark_serving.py
vllm.author: alexm-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This is a small PR to add tokenizer_mode param to benchmark_serving.py.
Example execution results are here:
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1biWEgyAyZ-WLis6KcyyNASfsLrsUSNNg2_wLpC6-4Hg/edit?usp=sharing

vllm.title: [Doc] Reorganize online pooling APIs
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR moves the /v1/score endpoint to /score to better indicate that the Score API is not part of OpenAI spec.
It also fixes some small issues in the pooling examples.
cc @maxdebayser

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Hardware][CPU] Enable Gemma2 with SDPA on CPU backend
vllm.author: janimo
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Make sure is_causal is only True when mask is None as required by SDPA otherwise there is an error raised
RuntimeError: _scaled_dot_product_attention: Explicit attn_mask should not be set when is_causal=True
With models such as Gemma2, that have alternating layers of sliding window attention and ful

vllm.title: [Distributed] Allow the placement group more time to wait for resources to be ready
vllm.author: Jeffwan
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #11134
Case 1: Waiting resource to be ready

start a ray cluster ray start --head --dashboard-host 0.0.0.0 --disable-usage-stats --block  --num-gpus 1
launch vLLM python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server     --model Qwen/Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct     --trust-remote-code     --distributed-executo

vllm.title: [Core] V1: Use multiprocessing by default
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR turns on VLLM_ENABLE_V1_MULTIPROCESSING by default.
It handles the choice of Python multiprocessing method in such a way that we
try to give things the best chance of working, and clear failures if not. This
topic is discussed in detail in the included design doc.
https://github.com/russellb

vllm.title: [V1][Bugfix] Fix EngineCoreProc profile
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Small bugfix when profiling in V1 with VLLM_ENABLE_V1_MULTIPROCESSING and TP size > 1

vllm.title: [Bugfix][V1] Re-compute an entire block when fully cache hit
vllm.author: comaniac
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Previously we re-compute the last token when the prompt is fully cache hit. However, since allocate_slots() calculates number of computed tokens based on the number of computed blocks, the number of computed tokens allocate_slots() inferred is always multiply by the block size. This results in the l
